_note: Plum-garden discussion of heroes (winter 199); Liu Bei accepts the belt-decree; Yuan Shu's last days; Liu Bei takes Xuzhou and kills Che Zhou.
Yuan Shao and Cao Cao both mobilize; opening exchanges of the Guandu campaign in early 200 (Liu Dai/Wang Zhong probe Liu Bei).
Mi Heng's naked diatribe against Cao Cao; Ji Ping's poison plot; the belt-decree conspiracy unravels. Spring 200.
Consort Dong murdered; Cao Cao attacks Liu Bei at Xuzhou; Liu Bei flees north to Yuan Shao (early 200).
Guan Yu cornered at Tushan accepts Cao Cao's terms; relieves Baima by slaying Yan Liang (April 200, Sanguozhi: Battle of Baima).
Wen Chou killed at Yan Ford (Battle of Yan Crossing, May 200); Guan Yu refuses Cao Cao's largesse and rides off.
Guan Yu's solitary northward journey, slaying six generals at five passes. Set in mid-200, en route to find Liu Bei.
Cai Yang slain at Gucheng; brothers reunited; Liu Bei consolidates at Runan. Late 200.
Frame opens with 'fourth year of Rebuilt Tranquillity (AD 199)' covering Lujiang's fall; Sun Ce killed by Xu Gong's clients (April 200); Sun Quan succeeds him.
Battle of Guandu proper — Cao Cao burns Wuchao's supplies (October 200); Ju Shou captured and executed.
